params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

params.description: Filter search jobs by search string

items.docs: Returns a list of current search jobs for the authenticated user. Includes job status, progress, and metadata for each job.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Creates a new search job. The search job runs asynchronously. Use the returned search ID (sid) to check job status and retrieve results. Searches use the Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL).

params.name: search_id
params.description: The search ID (sid) of the search job

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Returns detailed information about a specific search job including its status, progress, performance metrics, and configuration.

params.name: search_id
params.description: The search ID (sid) of the search job

items.docs: Cancels and deletes the specified search job and its results.

params.name: search_id
params.description: The search ID (sid) of the search job

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Execute a control action on a search job such as pause, unpause, finalize, cancel, or touch (extend the job lifetime).

params.name: search_id
params.description: The search ID (sid) of the search job

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

params.name: search
params.description: Post-processing search string to filter results

params.name: field_list
params.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return

items.docs: Returns the results of a completed search job. Results are available only after the search job has finished. Use the count and offset parameters for pagination.

params.name: search_id
params.description: The search ID (sid) of the search job

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

params.name: earliest_time
params.description: Earliest time boundary for events

params.name: latest_time
params.description: Latest time boundary for events

params.name: search
params.description: Post-processing search to filter events

params.name: field_list
params.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return

params.name: truncation_mode
params.description: How to truncate long lines

params.name: max_lines
params.description: Maximum number of lines per event

items.docs: Returns the untransformed events of a search job. Unlike results, events return the raw data before any transforming commands are applied. Available for searches that include non-transforming commands.

params.name: search
params.description: The SPL search query to execute

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: earliest_time
params.description: Earliest time for the search

params.name: latest_time
params.description: Latest time for the search

params.name: auto_cancel
params.description: Seconds of inactivity after which the search is cancelled

params.name: enable_lookups
params.description: Whether to enable lookups during the search

items.docs: Runs a search and streams results back as they become available, rather than waiting for the search to complete. This is a streaming endpoint suitable for long-running searches or real-time searches. The search runs synchronously and results stream back in the response.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

params.description: Filter indexes by name or properties

params.name: sort_dir
params.description: Sort direction

params.description: Filter by data type

items.docs: Returns a list of all indexes accessible to the authenticated user. Includes index configuration, storage paths, and data retention settings.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Creates a new index with the specified configuration. The index name must be unique and conform to Splunk naming conventions.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the index

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Returns detailed configuration and status information for a specific index including storage paths, retention settings, and current size.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the index

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Updates the configuration of an existing index. Not all index properties can be modified after creation.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the index

items.docs: Marks an index for deletion. The index data is removed according to the configured retention policy. Requires the admin role.

info.name: List File and Directory Monitor Inputs

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

params.name: search
params.description: Filter inputs by search string

items.docs: Returns a list of file and directory monitoring inputs. Monitor inputs continuously watch files and directories for new data.

info.name: Create a File or Directory Monitor Input

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Creates a new file or directory monitoring input. The specified path will be monitored for new data and ingested into the specified index.

params.name: name
params.description: The name (path) of the monitor input

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Returns configuration details for a specific file or directory monitoring input.

params.name: name
params.description: The name (path) of the monitor input

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Updates the configuration of an existing monitor input.

params.name: name
params.description: The name (path) of the monitor input

items.docs: Deletes the specified monitor input. Splunk stops monitoring the associated file or directory.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

items.docs: Returns a list of TCP cooked data inputs. Cooked TCP inputs receive data from Splunk forwarders.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

items.docs: Returns a list of raw TCP data inputs. Raw TCP inputs receive data directly over a TCP port without Splunk protocol framing.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

items.docs: Returns a list of UDP data inputs. UDP inputs receive data over a UDP port.

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

params.name: count
params.description: Maximum number of items to return. A value of 0 returns all items.

params.name: offset
params.description: Index of the first item to return. Used with count for pagination.

items.docs: Returns a list of HTTP Event Collector (HEC) tokens configured on the Splunk instance. Each token defines an input channel for receiving data over HTTP/HTTPS.

info.name: Create an Http Event Collector Token

params.name: output_mode
params.description: Response format

items.docs: Creates a new HTTP Event Collector (HEC) token for data ingestion over HTTP/HTTPS.

info.name: Send Events via Http Event Collector

items.docs: Sends one or more events to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector. Events are submitted as JSON objects. Multiple events can be sent in a single request by concatenating JSON objects.

info.name: Send Raw Data via Http Event Collector

params.name: channel
params.description: Channel identifier (GUID) for event ordering. Required if indexer acknowledgment is enabled.

params.name: sourcetype
params.description: Override the sourcetype for the event data

params.name: source
params.description: Override the source for the event data

params.name: host
params.description: Override the host for the event data

params.name: index
params.description: Override the destination index for the event data

items.docs: Sends raw event data to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector. The raw data is ingested as-is without requiring JSON formatting. Useful for log file data and other unstructured text.
Queries the indexer acknowledgment status for events sent via HTTP Event Collector. Returns whether events with specified ack IDs have been indexed.
